title: Megalastrum platylobum
intro: Megalastrum platylobum is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Megalastrum platylobum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Megalastrum platylobum include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and bronchitis.
